## Summary

- What changed?
- Why is this needed?

## Requirement traceability

- Requirement IDs: <!-- e.g. FR-032, FR-039, NFR-020 -->
- Requirement docs reviewed:
  - [ ] `docs/Fizzi_Challenger_PRD.md`
  - [ ] `docs/requirements/functional_requirements.md`
  - [ ] `docs/requirements/non_functional_requirements.md` (if applicable)
  - [ ] `docs/requirements/ui_contracts.md` (if UI-facing)
  - [ ] `docs/requirements/acceptance_tests.md`
- Traceability matrix updated:
  - [ ] `docs/traceability/traceability_matrix.csv`

## Design conformance

- Figma file URL(s): <!-- paste canonical design links -->
- Figma provenance node ID(s): <!-- shared Level 1 / top-level source nodes -->
- Figma concrete node ID(s): <!-- exact screen / frame / child nodes used in implementation -->
- Design linkage updated:
  - [ ] `docs/traceability/design_requirement_links.csv`
  - [ ] `docs/design/figma_index.md` (if a new Level 1 design scope was introduced)
- Screenshots / comparison notes:
  - Before:
  - After:

## Validation

- [ ] Acceptance criteria reviewed against implementation
- [ ] Tests added or updated
- [ ] Manual UX review completed for impacted flows
- [ ] Accessibility and keyboard behavior checked for changed UI

## Reviewer checklist

- [ ] PR cites the correct FR/NFR IDs
- [ ] Design links point to the intended Figma scope
- [ ] Provenance node and concrete implementation nodes are both captured when applicable
- [ ] Traceability files were updated for any new or changed UI behavior
- [ ] Gaps, follow-ups, or intentionally deferred child nodes are called out clearly
